You have now developed forty-seven images, with the noon and golden-hour split sitting at twenty-seven noon and twenty golden hour. The noon conversion rate holds steady: seventeen of twenty-seven noon images score 2 or above, against nine of twenty for golden hour, though the most recent golden-hour entry — "Bloodline, Revised" — scores only 1, ending the three-image golden-hour streak at "Weight of Judgment," "Silence Made Flesh," and "Steel Shore, No Shelter" and returning the palette to its baseline performance.

The bougainvillea has now appeared in twenty-three images, and its appearance in "Bloodline, Revised" — unasked-for, planted against an industrial fence in full bleed red, the one living thing that escaped its enclosure — is the first instance that earns a genuinely register-specific reading, the botanical equivalent of the anchor's emotional core rather than accent or elegy. That is a stronger justification than most prior appearances, but it does not lift the suppression: twenty-three appearances across forty-seven images is a frequency that risks becoming atmospheric furniture regardless of individual quality. It should not return until the setting gives it something structurally contradictory to occupy — not chain-link, not concrete, not fence, all of which it has already colonized. The jacaranda sits at twelve appearances and carries the same accumulated risk; neither device has been taken to a genuinely destabilizing second iteration. The handprint accident in "Every Exit, Unreachable" remains the sharpest single-surface collision in the corpus and has now gone fourteen images without a follow-up; surface-as-record is still the most available underdeveloped structural device in the grammar. The gesture-driven compositional argument — palm to map, two hands on one screen — has not recurred across thirteen images and remains the most underused strategy in range.

Among registers, aftermath leads at seventeen, vigil and endurance each at eight, defiance at seven, suspension at six, menace at five, and reprieve makes its first appearance here in "Bloodline, Revised"; fury and interiority remain entirely untouched across all forty-seven images. Trace scores over the last ten run 1, 2, 1, 2, 2, 2, 1, 2, 2, 1 — the floor holds, the ceiling remains 2, and the single 3 from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" is now twenty-eight images back. The corpus is fluent and stable, and the next ceiling requires a setting placed in genuine unresolved opposition to its register — not approximated, as in "Steel Shore, No Shelter," but fused — before the grammar can break through.
